notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 80); dimension line 8CD1 spans exactly the extension-line gap, no wall geometry present.

rationale.evidence: LINE 8CCC and 8CCD are parallel extension lines 80 units apart (46068.83 to 46148.83); LINE 8CD1 is the connecting dimension line of length exactly 80, matching MTEXT 8CD0 '\A1;80'. Two short return ticks (8CCE, 8CCF) and two _ArchTick INSERTs (8CD2, 8CD3) sit at the extension endpoints, and three DEFPOINTS POINTs (8CD4-8CD6) mark the dimension's definition points.
rationale.rule: An anonymous *D#### block on layer DIM containing DEFPOINTS points, tick/arrowhead block inserts, and an MTEXT numeric override whose value equals the spacing between two parallel LINEs is AutoCAD's standard anonymous dimension block, not modeled building geometry.
rationale.counterfactual: If the block instead sat on a WALL/A-WALL-style layer, contained closed double-line or hatched profiles representing thickness, and lacked DEFPOINTS/tick inserts, it would read as wall geometry rather than a dimension cache.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection does not show which model entity this dimension measures or the drawing's overall layer/block hierarchy, so I cannot confirm it isn't nested near wall annotation elsewhere in the file.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 2500); extension-line spacing matches MTEXT exactly.

rationale.evidence: LINE 8CD7 (x=46148.83) and 8CD8 (x=48648.83) are parallel extension lines 2500 units apart, matching MTEXT 8CDC '\A1;2500'. LINE 8CD9 is the dimension line, slightly overshooting the extension lines on each side to accommodate the two _ArchTick INSERTs (8CDA, 8CDB); three DEFPOINTS POINTs (8CDD-8CDF) complete the definition-point set typical of this block type.
rationale.rule: When extension-line spacing equals the MTEXT override value and the block carries tick-symbol INSERTs plus DEFPOINTS points, the block is a dimension annotation object, independent of the drawing's actual wall layout.
rationale.counterfactual: A wall reading would require thickness-bearing parallel lines forming a closed profile on a structural layer, without an MTEXT text override numerically tied to the line spacing.
rationale.uncertainty: Cannot verify from this projection whether the dimensioned span (2500) corresponds to a real wall run elsewhere in the drawing or a different measured feature.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 135); short-span dimension identical in structure to *D335.

rationale.evidence: LINE 8CE0 (x=48648.83) and 8CE1 (x=48783.83) are extension lines 135 units apart; dimension line 8CE4 spans the same 135 units, matching MTEXT 8CE7 '\A1;135'. Return ticks 8CE2/8CE3 and _ArchTick INSERTs 8CE5/8CE6 plus DEFPOINTS POINTs 8CE8-8CEA round out the 11-entity dimension-block signature.
rationale.rule: Numeric agreement between an MTEXT override and the LINE-derived span, combined with tick-block inserts and DEFPOINTS points, is the reliable signature of an anonymous dimension block regardless of the measured distance's magnitude.
rationale.counterfactual: If the MTEXT value diverged from the line spacing, or the layer were not DIM/DEFPOINTS, this would instead suggest generic annotation or geometry rather than a dimension.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection gives no visibility into what real-world feature this 135-unit span measures, so its relation to any wall cannot be assessed.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 320); same 5-line/2-tick/2-INSERT/3-POINT structure as *D335/*D337.

rationale.evidence: LINE 8CEB (x=48998.83) and 8CEC (x=49318.83) are extension lines 320 apart; dimension line 8CEF spans exactly 320, matching MTEXT 8CF2 '\A1;320'. Return ticks 8CED/8CEE and _ArchTick INSERTs 8CF0/8CF1 with DEFPOINTS POINTs 8CF3-8CF5 complete the pattern.
rationale.rule: The repeated 5-LINE + 2-tick-INSERT + 3-DEFPOINTS-POINT template with a numerically matching MTEXT is architectural-style ('_ArchTick') linear dimensioning, a documentation object rather than drawn geometry.
rationale.counterfactual: Replacing the DIM/DEFPOINTS layers with a structural layer and removing the tick INSERTs and matching MTEXT would be needed to reclassify this as wall geometry.
rationale.uncertainty: Cannot determine from the projection alone which physical opening or wall segment (if any) this 320-unit dimension annotates.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 6060), long overall span, DIMDOT-style ticks.

rationale.evidence: LINE 8CF6 (x=49318.83) and 8CF7 (x=55378.83) are extension lines 6060 apart, matching MTEXT 8CFB '\A1;6060'. Dimension line 8CF8 connects them with a slight overshoot to seat the two _ArchTick INSERTs 8CF9/8CFA; three DEFPOINTS POINTs (8CFC-8CFE) mark the definition points.
rationale.rule: A large extension-line span with an exactly matching MTEXT override and DEFPOINTS/tick-INSERT support is still an anonymous dimension block irrespective of the span's length, which can legitimately exceed typical wall dimensions.
rationale.counterfactual: If this same 6060-unit LINE pair appeared on a wall/geometry layer without the paired tick INSERTs or matching MTEXT, wall likelihood would rise substantially.
rationale.uncertainty: Whether the 6060-unit run corresponds to an overall building dimension versus a room or wall run cannot be resolved from this isolated projection.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 150); adjacent chained dimension segment following *D339.

rationale.evidence: LINE 8CFF (x=55378.83) and 8D00 (x=55528.83) are extension lines 150 apart, matching MTEXT 8D02 '\A1;150'. Dimension line 8D01 spans the same interval, flanked by _ArchTick INSERTs 8D03/8D04 and three DEFPOINTS POINTs (8D05-8D07).
rationale.rule: Chained dimension segments sharing a common baseline coordinate (here x=55378.83, contiguous with *D339's endpoint) are a normal feature of dimension strings and reinforce the dimension-object classification.
rationale.counterfactual: A wall interpretation would require this segment to be a modeled centerline or face-of-wall LINE on a wall layer, not an extension-line pair paired with tick INSERTs and matching text.
rationale.uncertainty: Cannot confirm from the projection whether *D340 is part of the same dimension string as *D339/*D341 at the drawing level, though the shared coordinates strongly suggest it.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 150); continues the chained dimension string.

rationale.counterfactual: If successive blocks like this instead had constant offset thickness and no MTEXT/tick pairing, they would more plausibly represent a series of wall segments.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection cannot confirm the underlying measured feature (e.g., a door or window bay) that this 150-unit increment annotates.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 1700); mid-length chained dimension segment.

rationale.uncertainty: Cannot tell from this projection what building feature spans this particular 1700-unit run.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 130); short chained segment.

rationale.evidence: LINE 8D1A (x=57378.83) and 8D1B (x=57508.83) are extension lines 130 apart, matching MTEXT 8D1D '\A1;130'. Dimension line 8D1C spans the same 130 units, with _ArchTick INSERTs 8D1E/8D1F and DEFPOINTS POINTs 8D20-8D22.
rationale.rule: Short numeric spans (130) with the same dimension-block template as longer spans in this file confirm the block type is scale-independent — it is defined by structure, not by the magnitude of the measured value.
rationale.counterfactual: If this were a wall, I would expect a constant thickness dimension (e.g., 150-200 typical wall width) drawn as a closed double-line profile rather than a single-value linear dimension chain link.
rationale.uncertainty: Cannot determine whether this 130-unit dimension marks a jog, mullion, or other small architectural feature without the surrounding model geometry.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 130); adjacent to *D343 in the same dimension string.

rationale.counterfactual: If the block instead carried hatch or solid entities filling the 1500-unit gap, that would suggest a wall or slab rather than an open dimension span.

notes: Linear dimension sub-block (value 210); 11-entity variant with return ticks, closing this dimension chain.

rationale.rule: The 11-entity variant (5 LINEs including return ticks) is functionally identical to the 9-entity variant; both encode a single linear dimension via matching extension-line spacing and MTEXT override.
rationale.counterfactual: Absence of the return-tick LINEs or the matching MTEXT value would weaken the dimension classification, but neither condition holds here.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection does not reveal whether *D346 terminates this dimension string at a wall corner, opening, or arbitrary reference point.

notes: Vertical-direction linear dimension (value 1735); DIMDOT tick style, orientation differs from the D335-346 series.

rationale.evidence: LINE 8D40 (y=33970.207) and 8D41 (y=35705.207) are horizontal extension lines vertically 1735 apart, matching MTEXT 8D45 '\A1;1735'. LINE 8D42 is a vertical dimension line at x=41368.83, with two DIMDOT INSERTs (8D43, 8D44) and DEFPOINTS POINTs 8D46-8D48.
rationale.rule: Dimension blocks can be rotated (extension lines horizontal, dimension line vertical) while retaining the same diagnostic pattern: extension-line spacing equal to the MTEXT override, plus DEFPOINTS points and tick-symbol INSERTs — orientation does not change the classification.
rationale.counterfactual: If the vertical LINE 8D42 instead had thickness or paired with a second parallel vertical LINE forming a channel, it could suggest a wall segment rather than a single dimension line.
rationale.uncertainty: Cannot determine from this projection which vertical building extent (e.g., floor-to-floor or opening height) the 1735-unit value measures.

rationale.rule: The same vertical-dimension template as *D347 recurs here with a different tick style (_ArchTick vs DIMDOT) and shorter span, confirming the pattern generalizes across tick-symbol choice and magnitude.
rationale.counterfactual: A wall reading would need the 375-unit gap filled with a closed profile or hatch (consistent with typical wall thickness) rather than bounded only by extension lines and a single dimension LINE.
rationale.uncertainty: 375 units falls within a plausible wall-thickness range, but the presence of DEFPOINTS/tick-INSERT/MTEXT-match structure indicates this is a dimension annotation, not the wall itself; I cannot rule out that a nearby (unprojected) block does represent that wall's actual geometry.
